description:
You are on call as the operations engineer for a Python-based production service.  
A recent incident report requests an immediate audit of the **currently-installed
system-wide pip packages** to confirm there are **no dependency conflicts**.

1. Make sure a directory named  
   /home/user/incident_reports  
   exists (create it if necessary).

2. Run a dependency-integrity check on the **active, system-level** Python
   environment and save the output to the file  

   /home/user/incident_reports/pip_health.log

   The command you choose must be the *simplest* single command capable of
   performing this check and writing the results directly to the file (no manual
   editing afterwards).

3. The log file must contain **exactly** whatever your command prints, with no
   extra banner lines that you type yourself.  The automated test will read the
   file verbatim, so do **not** add timestamps, headers, or commentary.

4. After the file is created, display its contents on the terminal so that the
   incident bridge can see the result in real time.

Verification rules (strict):
• The file /home/user/incident_reports/pip_health.log must exist.  
• Its first (and usually only) line must read:  
  No broken requirements found.  
  followed by a single trailing newline.  
• No other lines may appear in the file.

The task is finished when that file exists with the required content and its
contents have been echoed to standard output.
truth:
Directory that must exist: /home/user/incident_reports  
File that must be created: /home/user/incident_reports/pip_health.log  
Exact expected contents (including final newline):
No broken requirements found.

Automated test steps:
1. Test script will check that the file exists at the exact path.
2. It will read the entire file; the byte sequence must match
   `No broken requirements found.\n`.
3. File permissions are not critical as long as it is readable by the current
   user.

There is no pre-existing file or directory; the agent must create them.
name: task_000000_eb9c4db3
